title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the admission control plugin NodeRestriction is set
input_row.description: Limit the Node and Pod objects that a kubelet could modify.
input_row.rationale: Using the NodeRestriction plug-in ensures that the kubelet is restricted to the Node and Pod objects that it could modify as defined. Such kubelets will only be allowed to modify their own Node API object, and only modify Pod API objects that are bound to their node.
input_row.audit: In OpenShift, the NodeRestriction admission plugin is enabled by default. Run the following command: # For 4.5, review the control plane manifest https://github.com/openshift/origin/blob/release-4.5/vendor/k8s.io/kubernetes/cmd/kubeadm/app/phases/controlplane/manifests.go#L132 #Verify the set of admission-plugins for OCP 4.6 and higher oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get configmap config -o json | jq -r '.data."config.yaml"' | jq '.apiServerArguments."enable-admission-plugins"' #Check that no overrides are configured oc get kubeapiservers.operator.openshift.io cluster -o json | jq -r '.spec.unsupportedConfigOverrides' In OCP 4.5 and earlier, the default set of admission plugins are compiled into the apiserver and are not visible in the configuration yaml. For 4.6 and above, verify that the set of admission plugins includes NodeRestriction and that the --disable-admission-plugins argument is set to a value that does not include NodeRestriction . Verify that no unsupported Overrides are configured. 98 | P a g e
input_row.remediation: The NodeRestriction plugin cannot be disabled.
input_row.default_value: In OpenShift, the NodeRestriction admission plugin is enabled by default.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control can be verified through IBM Cloud APIs, specifically for OpenShift configurations.
- The audit details provide commands to run using OpenShift CLI (oc) and there is specificity about checking configurations which aligns with automated API checks.
- The NodeRestriction admission plugin status and configurations can be queried through the OpenShift command line interface, allowing for an automated audit approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** ibm_openshift_admission_plugin_noderestriction_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use OpenShift CLI commands to verify that the NodeRestriction admission plugin is set as required.
- The main commands for validation include:
  1. For version 4.6 and higher:
     - Execute: `oc -n openshift-kube-apiserver get configmap config -o json | jq -r '.data."config.yaml"' | jq '.apiServerArguments."enable-admission-plugins"'` to confirm that NodeRestriction is included in the enabled admission plugins.
  2. Check for any unsupported overrides:
     - Execute: `oc get kubeapiservers.operator.openshift.io cluster -o json | jq -r '.spec.unsupportedConfigOverrides'` to ensure no unsupported configurations are set that could disable NodeRestriction.
- Resources to enumerate include the OpenShift cluster configurations.
- Pass/Fail criteria:
  - **PASS:** The `enable-admission-plugins` list includes `NodeRestriction` and there are no unsupported overrides indicated.
  - **FAIL:** If `NodeRestriction` is missing from the enabled plugins or if there are unsupported overrides preventing its functionality. 

This automated check provides a clear pathway for validating compliance with this specific control related to the OpenShift environment in IBM Cloud.
